pattern: Sliding a single cut point across a 0/1 array and updating a running left-zero-count and right-one-count as the cut moves turns an O(n^2) rescan into one linear sweep.
hints: A cut at position i only cares about how many zeros sit to its left and how many ones sit to its right -- both of those counts change in a simple, predictable way as you slide the cut one step to the right.

Start with the cut at the very left (nothing on the left side yet, everything on the right), track the total count of ones up front, then walk the cut across the array updating a left-zero-count and a right-one-count incrementally instead of recomputing them.

Track the best score seen so far and the list of cut positions that reached it; whenever a new cut beats the best, reset that list, and whenever a cut ties it, append -- since you sweep left to right the list ends up sorted for free.

edge_inputs.description: all zeros, so the best cut keeps everything on the left
all ones, so the best cut keeps everything on the right
single element that is a zero
single element that is a one
strict alternation, producing several tied non-adjacent cut positions
a tie between the very first and very last cut positions
a single stray zero in the middle of ones on both sides
the best cut lands strictly inside the array rather than at either end

stress.description: large random binary array up to the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
